message:
chore(beads): close ft-xdt8j — NaN clamp guard fixed in ec0160d6

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -491,7 +491,7 @@
 {"id":"ft-xbnl0.5.5","title":"Assemble the final closure bundle, remaining-risk register, and release-candidate checklist","description":"# Goal
Package the finish-line work into one auditable closure bundle.

This task exists because the final state of the project should be easy to evaluate later without reconstructing the whole campaign from chat logs or scattered issue comments.","design":"# Design
1. Collect the evidence matrix, release gates, acceptance artifacts, docs updates, and major closure comments into one bead-native bundle.
2. Record any remaining risks or explicitly deferred work so the release candidate is honest.
3. Define the release-candidate checklist that future maintainers can reuse.
4. Ensure the bundle points back to the beads that generated the evidence instead of duplicating everything.","acceptance_criteria":"1. A future maintainer can evaluate the finish-line campaign from this bundle without reopening the old markdown plan.
2. Remaining risks or deferrals are explicit.
3. The release-candidate checklist is concrete and reusable.
4. The bundle serves as the final closure reference for this whole program.
5. The bundle indexes the unit, integration, and E2E scripts, remote `rch` commands, artifact locations, diagnostic surfaces, and first-run or recovery pathways generated by the program instead of summarizing them vaguely.","notes":"This is the capstone bead. It should read like the final answer to whether the project is actually over the goal line.

Its bundle must index the canonical shared contract in `docs/ft-xbnl0-verification-contract.md` before it summarizes downstream evidence.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cod3","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:22:33.624570Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","updated_at":"2026-04-19T21:05:02.429361Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T21:05:02.429066Z","close_reason":"Final closure bundle, metadata, and completion-evidence docs added","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["closure","evidence","goal-line","release-readiness"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.5","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:46.535721Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.5","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:22:33.624570Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.5","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:46.988053Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.5","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:47.443110Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.5","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:47.926843Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.5","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:48.382488Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.5","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:41:11.253093Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.5","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T20:08:13.693797Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-xbnl0.5.6","title":"Expose live health, leak, and backpressure diagnostics through doctor, status, and robot surfaces","description":"# Goal
Make long-haul swarm health visible to operators and other agents without requiring ad hoc tracing sessions or profiler archaeology.

This task exists because instrumentation only helps users once the important signals are surfaced through the product in a stable, interpretable way. A polished finish line needs truthful, actionable health diagnostics for day-2 operations, not just internal counters buried in logs.","design":"# Design
1. Surface the signals created by instrumentation and release-gate work through operator-facing commands and machine-facing endpoints.
2. Present both concise human summaries and structured machine-readable output so humans and agents share the same truth.
3. Include the counters and state that matter for long-haul swarm operation: pane, tab, or session counts, reconnect or shutdown anomalies, backpressure or queue pressure, leak-suspicion signals, and guidance on when to escalate.
4. Back the surface with comprehensive unit coverage and at least one logged incident-style E2E scenario.
5. Keep the semantics stable enough that playbooks, docs, and acceptance scenarios can depend on them.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Operators can inspect key long-haul health signals without attaching a profiler or reading raw logs.
2. Human and machine surfaces expose the same authoritative health state with clear field semantics.
3. Comprehensive unit tests cover aggregation, thresholding, serialization, and degraded-state handling for the new diagnostics surfaces.
4. At least one deterministic E2E or incident-rehearsal script exercises normal and degraded paths with detailed timestamped logs and stored artifacts.
5. Playbooks, docs, and operator acceptance scenarios are updated to use these surfaces as the standard diagnostic entry point.","notes":"This bead turns internal reliability work into something operators can actually use when a swarm starts to misbehave under long-haul load.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:37:15.364320Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-06T00:01:12.386236Z","closed_at":"2026-04-06T00:01:12.386032Z","close_reason":"Implemented HealthDiagnosticsData, HealthLeakRiskData, HealthGuidance types in robot_types.rs with from_health_snapshot() builder. Added Health variant to ApiSurface enum. 21 comprehensive unit tests covering all health levels (green/yellow/red/black), backpressure tiers, fleet pressure tiers, crash loops, leak risk fields, lock contention, guidance generation, serde roundtrip, and envelope roundtrip. All 51 robot_api_contracts + health diagnostics tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["diagnostics","e2e-tests","goal-line","operator","reliability","robot-mode","test-coverage","test-logging"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.6","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:41:06.720102Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.6","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:41:08.319158Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.6","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:37:15.364320Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-xbnl0.5.7","title":"Polish first-run setup, environment validation, and recovery entrypoints for operators","description":"# Goal
Make the project pleasant and trustworthy from the first real operator interaction: initial setup, environment validation, first successful control-plane run, and entry into recovery workflows.

This task exists because `ft-xbnl0.5.4` proves fresh-install and recovery scenarios, but the plan did not yet contain a dedicated implementation bead that improves those entrypoints themselves. A polished finish line should not merely detect that setup and recovery are rough; it should explicitly make them operator-friendly.","design":"# Design
1. Improve the first-run path so operators can get from clone or install to a validated, ready-to-use FrankenTerm workspace without reading source code or guessing about prerequisites.
2. Make environment validation and doctor output actionable, including missing dependency diagnosis, configuration bootstrap issues, restore-state problems, and recovery entrypoints.
3. Ensure the first-run and recovery surfaces point naturally into the new diagnostic, playbook, and acceptance tooling instead of acting like separate subsystems.
4. Back the flow with comprehensive unit coverage for setup or validation logic plus deterministic E2E scenarios that exercise clean-start, broken-environment, and recovery-entry cases with detailed logs and stored artifacts.
5. Keep the result honest: if some setup or recovery path is intentionally manual, document it clearly and make the product surface say so directly.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Operators can move from clean environment to validated first successful run using a documented and product-supported path rather than tribal knowledge.
2. Missing prerequisites, bad config, restore-state issues, and other common startup failures produce actionable doctor or status output with clear next steps.
3. Recovery entrypoints route operators toward the standard diagnostic and playbook surfaces rather than leaving them in ad hoc failure states.
4. Comprehensive unit tests cover setup-state detection, degraded-environment handling, and operator-facing error classification for the touched surfaces.
5. At least one deterministic E2E scenario exercises clean setup, one exercises a broken-environment or bad-config path, and one exercises recovery entry, all with detailed timestamped logging, artifact capture, and exact verification commands recorded.","notes":"This bead makes fresh-install and recovery usability an explicit implementation concern rather than something only checked at the very end by acceptance scenarios.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-03T20:08:11.049428Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-19T17:15:33.968982Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T17:15:33.968636Z","close_reason":"operator guidance landed; artifact docs/ft-xbnl0-5-7-completion-evidence.md","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["doctor","e2e-tests","goal-line","operator","recovery","setup","test-coverage","test-logging"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.7","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T20:08:12.565115Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.7","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T20:08:11.049428Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.7","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T20:08:11.711759Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.7","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T20:08:12.253037Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"ft-xdt8j","title":"[bug][resource-types] backpressure_severity propagates NaN through clamp() — observe_ratio(NaN) corrupts smoothed_ratio; from_severity(NaN) leaks NaN through every output field","description":"Discovered via proptest in frankenterm-core-resource-types (AmberHare 2026-04-26). Two related defects:

1. ContinuousBackpressure::observe_ratio (backpressure_severity.rs:140) calls raw_ratio.clamp(0.0, 1.0). Rust's f64::clamp returns NaN for NaN input — it does NOT map NaN to a saturation bound. Folding NaN into the EMA poisons smoothed_ratio() permanently (NaN never decays). severity() still survives because sigmoid() has explicit NaN→0.5 handling, but operator-facing readouts (smoothed_ratio observability) become NaN.

2. ThrottleActions::from_severity (backpressure_severity.rs:88) does s = severity.clamp(0.0, 1.0); for NaN input s is NaN. Then s*s, 0.25*s, 1.0+3.0*s, 1.0-0.8*s are all NaN. Every output field violates its documented [0,1]/[1,4]/etc. range.

Reproducer: cargo test -p frankenterm-core-resource-types --test proptest_backpressure_severity -- continuous_observe_keeps_invariants throttle_actions_clamps_every_output (currently the proptest avoids NaN inputs to keep CI green; un-skip by re-adding Just(f64::NAN) to arb_ratio_sequence/arb_severity_adversarial).

Recommended fix: replace .clamp(0.0, 1.0) with an explicit NaN-aware clamp:
  let safe = if x.is_nan() { 0.0 } else { x.clamp(0.0, 1.0) };
in both observe_ratio and from_severity. Same pattern is already present in sigmoid() (returns 0.5 for NaN), so the cluster is partly hardened but not consistently.

Severity: P2 — operator dashboards can display NaN; throttling logic is unaffected because sigmoid clamps.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T22:10:08.091800Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T22:10:08.091800Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"ft-xdt8j","title":"[bug][resource-types] backpressure_severity propagates NaN through clamp() — observe_ratio(NaN) corrupts smoothed_ratio; from_severity(NaN) leaks NaN through every output field","description":"Discovered via proptest in frankenterm-core-resource-types (AmberHare 2026-04-26). Two related defects:

1. ContinuousBackpressure::observe_ratio (backpressure_severity.rs:140) calls raw_ratio.clamp(0.0, 1.0). Rust's f64::clamp returns NaN for NaN input — it does NOT map NaN to a saturation bound. Folding NaN into the EMA poisons smoothed_ratio() permanently (NaN never decays). severity() still survives because sigmoid() has explicit NaN→0.5 handling, but operator-facing readouts (smoothed_ratio observability) become NaN.

2. ThrottleActions::from_severity (backpressure_severity.rs:88) does s = severity.clamp(0.0, 1.0); for NaN input s is NaN. Then s*s, 0.25*s, 1.0+3.0*s, 1.0-0.8*s are all NaN. Every output field violates its documented [0,1]/[1,4]/etc. range.

Reproducer: cargo test -p frankenterm-core-resource-types --test proptest_backpressure_severity -- continuous_observe_keeps_invariants throttle_actions_clamps_every_output (currently the proptest avoids NaN inputs to keep CI green; un-skip by re-adding Just(f64::NAN) to arb_ratio_sequence/arb_severity_adversarial).

Recommended fix: replace .clamp(0.0, 1.0) with an explicit NaN-aware clamp:
  let safe = if x.is_nan() { 0.0 } else { x.clamp(0.0, 1.0) };
in both observe_ratio and from_severity. Same pattern is already present in sigmoid() (returns 0.5 for NaN), so the cluster is partly hardened but not consistently.

Severity: P2 — operator dashboards can display NaN; throttling logic is unaffected because sigmoid clamps.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T22:10:08.091800Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T22:18:45.783259Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T22:18:45.782987Z","close_reason":"Fixed in ec0160d6: explicit is_nan() guard in observe_ratio and from_severity maps NaN to least-throttled (0.0). Companion proptest re-enables NaN in adversarial strategies — 7/7 proptests + 97/97 lib tests green.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-xgs7n","title":"[testing-fuzzing] pin extract_delta overlap-existence invariant: previous.ends_with(current[..len-delta.len()])","description":"# Gap

proptest_ingest_extract_delta_fuzz.rs covers 5 MRs (crash-freedom,
identity, content-suffix, pure-append, determinism) + 2 inline
regression tests, but does NOT pin the primary semantic contract of
the algorithm: **when Content(delta) is returned, the non-delta head
of `current` must be a tail of `previous`**. Formally:

    extract_delta(previous, current, _) = Content(delta)
    ⇒ previous.ends_with(&current[..current.len() - delta.len()])

This is the invariant the sliding-window search ACTUALLY proves
(search_window[pos..] == current[..overlap_len], where
search_window = previous[search_start..], so previous ends with
current[..overlap_len]). Without it, the algorithm could return a
Content(delta) where the "non-delta prefix" is pure fabrication —
the existing MR3 suffix-check proves current ends with delta but
says nothing about the overlap being real.

Adding this test catches any regression in the overlap-candidate
acceptance logic (`search_window[pos..] == current[..overlap_len]`
on ingest.rs:1711) that accidentally accepts a non-matching window.

Fix: new proptest in proptest_ingest_extract_delta_fuzz.rs covering
every path (pure-append fast path + sliding-window search).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-22T10:04:40.819353Z","created_by":"pane3-ingest-mr","updated_at":"2026-04-22T10:13:32.759851Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T10:13:32.759521Z","close_reason":"Landed in 5eb9f956 (fix(apc, backpressure, ingest): …, overlap-existence proptest) — extract_delta_content_overlap_exists_in_previous added to tests/proptest_ingest_extract_delta_fuzz.rs:207. Verified: 1 passed / 0 failed / 14 filtered out on --test proptest_ingest_extract_delta_fuzz (test run took 0.20s under 512 proptest cases). Pins the semantic contract that when Content(delta) returns, previous.ends_with(current[..current.len() - delta.len()]) — the 'overlap was real' claim that MR3's suffix-only check does not catch.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ingest","proptest","testing"]}
 {"id":"ft-xicum","title":"session_restore delete_session returns false after cleaning orphaned checkpoint rows","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"pane8","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:50:56.388120Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:52:53.130618Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T11:52:53.130299Z","close_reason":"delete_session now returns true when it removed orphaned checkpoint or pane-state rows, not just when the parent mux_sessions row existed; added regression coverage for orphan cleanup.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-xkpy1","title":"[ux][P3] Robot + MCP error-hint audit follow-up (beyond MCP_ERR_POLICY)","description":"## Context

/ux-audit sweep of robot + MCP surfaces. Primary finding closed as a trivial ship: all 7 MCP_ERR_POLICY deny sites in mcp_tools.rs (6 McpToolError::new sites + 1 McpEnvelope in the mcp_authorize_mcp_mutation gate helper) previously passed None as hint — leaving MCP clients with 'Read denied by policy' and nowhere to go. Now all seven route through a shared POLICY_DENY_HINT const naming two actionable recovery paths (config.safety.rules for operator review, policy_denied_audit table for decision context).

Secondary findings not in that ship, each worth a separate pass:

## 1. Robot error-code coverage drift vs README

Defined in crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs:4549-4564:
  robot.invalid_args, robot.unknown_subcommand, robot.config_error,
  robot.fts_query_error, robot.approval_error, robot.pane_not_found,
  robot.reservation_conflict, robot.storage_error, robot.feature_not_available,
  robot.policy_denied, robot.timeout, robot.workflow_aborted,
  robot.workflow_error, robot.workflow_not_found, robot.not_implemented,
  robot.remote_text_unavailable

README.md §Error Handling (robot mode) lists robot.wezterm_not_running and robot.require_approval — neither is a defined ROBOT_ERR_* constant. Either the README is stale (likely) or those codes exist under a different name (possible). Audit the README table against the actual constants + per-subcommand emission paths, update whichever is drifted.

## 2. Robot-mode hint uniformity

Walk every  construction in main.rs. MCP_ERR_POLICY just got treated; robot.pane_not_found, robot.timeout, robot.feature_not_available, etc. may have similar 'no-hint' patterns. Check each, add actionable hints matching the /ux-audit test: 'say what to do, not just what went wrong.'

## 3. Format-flag claims (toon vs json)

README claims  saves '40-60% fewer tokens' for AI-to-AI use. Spot-verify: render a few representative envelopes in both formats, measure actual byte counts, confirm the claim holds within some band. If TOON is actually ~15% smaller on small envelopes, the marketing text should be tightened.

## 4. Success-response conciseness

Every MCP tool envelope carries mcp_version, version, ok, data, elapsed_ms, now — 6 top-level keys. For small data payloads (e.g. a 2-item events list) the envelope can be 3-4x the size of the actual data. Consider whether any of mcp_version / version / now are redundant for the client once an initialize handshake has happened; file a separate architectural bead if so.

## Acceptance Criteria per item

- Item 1: README drift closed (either codes renamed in docs, or missing constants added in main.rs), with an assertion test that every documented code exists as a const.
- Item 2: Every robot-mode error construction reviewed; at least one shared const (like POLICY_DENY_HINT) for recurring hint families.
- Item 3: Measurement + either README update or TOON serializer improvement if claim is wrong.
- Item 4: Design doc; no code change until the redundancy is confirmed.

Surfaced by RusticMeadow during /ux-audit sweep, 2026-04-23.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"jemanuel","owner":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-23T21:24:05.956479Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T21:46:00.311456Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T21:42:29.408090Z","close_reason":"Partial: primary MCP_ERR_POLICY hint fix shipped as ride-along in c8252b9d (POLICY_DENY_HINT const applied to 7 deny sites). Four secondary audit items (robot.* code drift vs README, robot-mode hint uniformity, toon vs json format-claim measurement, envelope conciseness) enumerated in the bead body remain as concrete open follow-ups — each can be picked up independently. Not closing as-verified because those 4 haven't been verified or touched.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["hints","mcp","robot","ux"],"comments":[{"id":3628,"issue_id":"ft-xkpy1","author":"jemanuel","text":"Shipped in 65ea21d5. Scoped fix: robot read/search approval failures now share ROBOT_APPROVAL_RECOVERY_HINT across state include-text, get-text single, get-text multi, wait-for, and search. Verification partial: targeted bin test robot_approval_recovery_hint_mentions_watch_and_approve started but full ft binary test compile was still in progress at ship cutoff.","created_at":"2026-04-23T21:46:00Z"}]}
